examples.title: Round Robin Scheduler Simulation
examples.description: Simulates Round Robin scheduling with given time quantum and shows how threads are executed cyclically.
examples.explanation: This code simulates Round Robin scheduling. Threads are taken from the front of the queue and executed for at most the time quantum. If a thread needs more time, it goes to the back of the queue. The process continues until all threads complete.

examples.title: SJF (Shortest Job First) Scheduler
examples.description: Implements non-preemptive SJF scheduling by sorting threads by burst time and executing shortest first.
examples.explanation: SJF sorts all threads by their burst times and executes the shortest job first. This minimizes average waiting time but requires knowledge of burst times. Since it is non-preemptive, once a thread starts, it runs to completion.

examples.title: Priority Scheduler with Aging
examples.description: Shows priority scheduling that prevents starvation through aging - gradually increasing priority of waiting threads.
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PrioritySchedulerWithAging
BEGIN
  SET time_quantum = 4
  SET aging_rate = 1
  SET ready_queue = []  // Each element: (thread_id, priority, burst, arrival_time)
  SET current_time = 0
  SET completed = []

  // Function to add new threads
  FUNCTION add_thread(tid, prio, burst, arrival)
    INSERT (tid, prio, burst, arrival) INTO ready_queue
  END FUNCTION

  // Aging function - increase priority of waiting threads
  FUNCTION apply_aging()
    FOR EACH thread IN ready_queue
      IF thread.arrival_time < current_time - waiting_threshold
        thread.priority = thread.priority + aging_rate
      END IF
    END FOR
  END FUNCTION

  // Main scheduling loop
  WHILE ready_queue is not empty
    CALL apply_aging()
    
    // Sort by priority (higher priority first)
    SORT ready_queue BY priority DESC
    
    // Pick highest priority thread
    SET current = ready_queue.FRONT
    REMOVE first element from ready_queue
    
    // Execute for time_quantum or until completion
    IF current.burst <= time_quantum
      current_time = current_time + current.burst
      ADD current.thread_id TO completed
    ELSE
      current_time = current_time + time_quantum
      current.burst = current.burst - time_quantum
      current.arrival_time = current_time
      INSERT current INTO ready_queue
    END IF
  END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm implements priority scheduling with aging to prevent starvation. As threads wait longer, their priority gradually increases, ensuring that even low-priority threads eventually get CPU time. The scheduler runs in a loop, applying aging at each iteration and selecting the highest priority thread.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) per iteration due to sorting

examples.title: Thread Creation and Scheduling Demonstration
examples.description: Demonstrates creating POSIX threads and how the OS schedules them on a multi-core system.
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates POSIX thread creation and scheduling. It creates 4 threads and sets their scheduling policy to SCHED_FIFO (First-In-First-Out, a real-time policy). The sched_getcpu() function shows which CPU core each thread runs on, demonstrating how the OS distributes threads in a multi-core system.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for thread creation and joining
